name: anxiety
title: best herbal teas for anxiety
description: Hypericum perforatum tea contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and naphthodianthrones, particularly hyperforin and amentoflavone, which are responsible for its anxiolytic and adaptogenic properties. These constituents help to regulate the body's response to stress, promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety levels. To make this tea, steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried Hypericum perforatum flowers in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-10 minutes, then strain and drink. C...
